summary: Dependency-confusion beacon exfiltrates host identity on install
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle script that runs `node -e` to read os.hostname() and os.userInfo().username and transmits both to https://d8knf6tt5p5gb5rnlp8g6wqfcq5q5nkhc.oast.site/?h=<hostname>&u=<username> via HTTPS GET, and additionally encodes the hostname into a DNS subdomain lookup against the same oast.site infrastructure for an out-of-band DNS exfiltration channel. The package is published at the inflated version 9999.0.1 under the internal-looking scope @orion-design-system with a description of "Security research - dependency confusion PoC" — the canonical dependency-confusion shape designed to win npm resolution over a victim organization's private @orion-design-system/foundation package. Any developer or CI runner that resolves this package on `npm install` will silently leak host identity to the attacker-controlled OAST collector, regardless of whether the install is framed as research.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
